interview.why: Change and its two open decisions (cold-start/offline, migration of existing installs) are already articulated; interview only residual gaps (revocation, key rotation, integrity-over-artifact).

feasibility.why: Directional decision, not viability; one angle worth confirming: build-vs-buy on hosting (GitHub Releases vs npm/OCI registry vs bespoke).

prd.why: New FRs (hosted catalog repo, built+signed artifacts, plugin de-bundling, vestigial Role-toggle removal) on a shipped parent; author a fresh sibling PRD with a Parent-spec back-reference.

prototype.why: Re-added by user: prototype the changed UI surfaces (marketplace install/verify/offline states, the corrected errored banner, the Components editor without the legacy Role toggle).

architecture.why: Security-sensitive distribution re-platforming (signing, integrity-over-artifact, revocation, key rotation, offline/cold-start, install build step, migration) warrants full candidate exploration.

test-cases.why: New trust-critical surface (built+signed artifact verification, hosted-repo install, de-bundling, migration) needs full per-area authoring against the new PRD.

breakdown.why: Multi-component re-platforming (catalog repo split, install build step, plugin moves, UI deletion, ErroredBanner fix) decomposes into a fresh vertical-slice issue map.

review.why: Pruned: no implementation exists yet for this increment, and flow never runs review pre-implementation.

align.why: Reconcile the sibling's authored prd/architecture/test-cases/breakdown for cross-artifact consistency and confirm the Parent-spec back-reference.

scope.classification_rationale: All 33 issues (#598-#630) in .specifications/component-plugins/issues.json are CLOSED and gh resolved their state determinately, so the parent spec is shipped (stage 'align', full v1/v2/v3 artifact set including a pre-designed v3 marketplace phase). Case B: parent is read-only; included stages author into this sibling folder via branch-new.
